hardConstraints: No exercises or practice tasks here.
No free production. Input and explanation only.

A1.guidance: Isolated vocabulary with L1 translations. Clear grammar paradigms with minimal metalanguage. Max 8-10 new items. L1 support appropriate. Use simple, concrete examples the student can relate to.

A2.guidance: Vocabulary in short contextual sentences. Grammar through noticing tasks: present examples, elicit the rule. Short authentic-style texts (50-80 words) as input.

B1.guidance: Vocabulary via authentic-ish excerpts (100-150 words). Grammar through guided discovery: present data, ask student to formulate the rule. Include typical L1 interference notes.

B2.guidance: Register-appropriate authentic texts. Grammar nuance: indicative vs subjunctive, aspect, discourse markers. Vocabulary in extended context. Student formulates rules from data.

C1.guidance: Collocations, idioms, pragmatic functions, register shifts. Ungraded authentic texts. Grammar: pragmatic and sociolinguistic nuance. Student analyses and categorises independently.

C2.guidance: Collocations, idioms, pragmatic functions at near-native level. Authentic texts without grading. Mediation, paraphrase, register transfer as input tasks. Student leads analysis.
